title: TMS-EEG-MRI-fMRI-DWI data on paired associative stimulation and connectivity (Shirley Ryan AbilityLab, Chicago, IL)
description: This dataset comprises multimodal neuroimaging data from a bifocal transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) study investigating cortico-cortical paired associative stimulation (ccPAS) and its effects on interhemispheric motor cortex connectivity. Participants underwent TMS-EEG recordings targeting bilateral primary motor cortex with varying interstimulus intervals (4, 9, and 14 ms), combined with structural and functional MRI acquisitions. The dataset includes 64-channel EEG recordings, resting-state fMRI, high-angular-resolution diffusion imaging, and anatomical MRI to characterize spike-timing-dependent plasticity mechanisms in human motor networks.
methods_description: EEG was recorded from 64 channels in DC mode (reference: right mastoid) with electrode locations digitized for source analysis. Bifocal MRI-navigated TMS targeted the first dorsal interossei representation in bilateral primary motor cortex. Paired associative stimulation consisted of left M1 stimulation at t=0 ms followed by right M1 stimulation at asynchronies of 4, 9, or 14 ms across three sessions. Single-pulse TMS at 100% or 110% resting motor threshold was delivered before, 10 minutes after, and 60 minutes after ccPAS. Resting-state EEG (eyes open, fixation) was collected at baseline, pre-ccPAS, and post-ccPAS timepoints. MRI protocols included T1-weighted structural imaging, resting-state fMRI, and HARDI diffusion imaging (SMS 271 directions) in subsets of participants.

funding_references.funder_name: Dr. Ralph and Marian Falk Medical Research Trust
